# NCE BGA-2500 Draft Ball Map — READ THIS FIRST

**File:** `NCE_BGA2500_DRAFT_BALL_MAP.csv`
**Status:** DRAFT-PENDING-IP-OWNER-SIGNOFF (every row)
**Generated by:** `99_generation_scripts/generate_nce_draft_ball_map.py`
**Date:** 2026-07-10

## What this is

This is a **systematic engineering placeholder**, not an authoritative
vendor or foundry ball map. The LightRail NCE Gen3 "SpikingBrain" die
(U1/U4 on the schematic, footprint `LightRail:BGA-2500_40x40mm_P0.8mm`,
BOM MPN `LR-NCE-G3-BGA2500-001`) is LightRail's own proprietary ASIC and
**has not been taped out**. No real ball-out exists anywhere for this
part. This file was generated by allocating a standard 50x50 JEDEC-style
grid (2500 positions) into signal zones proportioned from LightRail's own
architecture documents (22-layer stack, dual HBM channel groups, TFLN/
optical SerDes lanes, PCIe/fabric fanout, and a power/ground/control
perimeter) — it is a scaffold for engineering review, nothing more.

## What it is NOT

- It is **not** a real ball map from any foundry, package house, or ASIC
  design-services vendor.
- It does **not** reflect actual signal integrity, power delivery, or
  thermal analysis.
- It must **never** be treated as final or used to generate real
  fabrication data (Gerbers, drill files, pick-and-place) as-is.

## Who needs to review/correct this

Bola Olatunji (IP owner of the NCE ASIC) or LightRail's ASIC design-
services partner, once one is engaged (candidates researched: GUC/Global
Unichip, Alchip, Faraday Technology — see
`11_production_fab_roadmap/PART_SOURCING_DECISIONS_2026-07-10.md`).

## Zone summary (2500 balls total)

| Category | Count | % | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| HBM_CHANNEL | 667 | 26.7% | Maps toward HBM stack positions U30-U35/U40-U45 |
| NC_RESERVED | 609 | 24.4% | Unassigned/padding — largest single bucket, deliberately conservative |
| OPTICAL_SERDES | 352 | 14.1% | Maps toward TFLN PIC at U3 |
| PCIE_FABRIC | 308 | 12.3% | Maps toward PEX88096 switches U270-U273 |
| POWER | 286 | 11.4% | Outer ring + alternating third ring |
| GROUND | 188 | 7.5% | Second ring |
| CTRL | 90 | 3.6% | Clock/reset/strap/debug, alternating third ring |

## Standing rule (do not remove this section)

Per the explicit instruction from Bola Olatunji: this ball map may never be
promoted to "final" or used as the basis for a fab-ready claim by an agent.
The board's fab-readiness status file may not be changed to
ready/approved until this file's status column changes from
`DRAFT-PENDING-IP-OWNER-SIGNOFF` to an IP-owner-approved state.
